# Korea Investment & Securities (KIS) Open Trading API

> Python examples and utilities for the KIS Open API, supporting automated trading, market data retrieval, account management, and strategy development.

## Core Modules & Examples

- [Examples for LLMs](examples_llm/): Atomic, single-function API examples optimized for AI-assisted code generation, validation, and endpoint-level understanding. Includes the authentication module (`kis_auth.py`) used throughout the examples.
- [Examples for Users](examples_user/): Comprehensive end-to-end workflow examples categorized by asset class (e.g., domestic stock, overseas stock, futures). Includes the authentication module (`kis_auth.py`) and demonstrates complete trading processes via multiple API calls.

## Configuration & Guides

- [Configuration Template](kis_devlp.yaml): Provides a template for environment configuration, including sandbox (paper trading) and production environments, API credentials (App Keys/Secrets) and account settings.
- [System Requirements & Setup](README.md): Main documentation including setup instructions. The project requires Python 3.11+ and recommends using the `uv` package manager.

## Advanced Strategy & Backtesting

- [Strategy Builder](strategy_builder/): Visual, no-code strategy builder and execution engine that generates Python code and exports a `.kis.yaml` file for backtesting.
- [Backtester](backtester/): QuantConnect Lean-based backtesting engine to validate `.kis.yaml` file-based strategies with historical market data and performance metrics.

## Official KIS Resources

- [KIS Developers Portal](https://apiportal.koreainvestment.com): Official developers portal for KIS Open API, including API documentation, authentication guides, developer tools, and support resources.
- [KIS AI Extensions](https://github.com/koreainvestment/kis-ai-extensions): MCP-based plugins enabling natural language control of the KIS Open API pipeline (strategy design → backtesting → order execution) via Claude, Cursor, Codex and Gemini.

## AI Agent Guidance

- Prefer `examples_llm/` for endpoint-level implementations.
- Use `examples_user/` for complete trading workflows.
- Reuse existing authentication helpers when available.
- Follow request structures and parameter conventions demonstrated in the examples.
